scope: R33 is rejected only as the first Auto-G16 transition-metal smoke candidate because its candidate-bound P1/M1 prerequisites cannot be closed from the reviewed evidence. This is not a rejection of the publication, benchmark set, reaction, or candidate's general chemical value.
grounds: Exact +1 charge and a source-reported closed-shell singlet calculation setting do not establish an accepted electronic-state model.
Formal oxidation state, ligand charge bookkeeping, d-electron count, alternative spin surfaces, wavefunction and stability policy remain unresolved.
Exact ligand and coordination identities, reactant and product identities, chemical atom mapping, intended reaction coordinate, normal-mode evidence, and path evidence remain unresolved.
Candidate-bound method, basis, ECP, relativity, solvent, thermochemistry, and transition-state strategy provenance remain unresolved.
The existing asymmetric M1 builder prerequisites cannot be truthfully supplied for this non-asymmetric benchmark without inferred or synthetic chemistry.
